quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Linked genes"?

options.text: One gene affects the expression of another gene at a different locus (e.g., coat color in Labrador retrievers: B/b for color, E/e for deposition)
The 4-carbon molecule that accepts acetyl-CoA to begin each turn of the Krebs cycle; regenerated at the end of each turn
The totality of an organism's chemical reactions; catabolism (breakdown, releases energy) and anabolism (synthesis, requires energy)
Genes located on the same chromosome that tend to be inherited together; do not assort independently

quiz_questions.explanation: "Linked genes" is defined as: Genes located on the same chromosome that tend to be inherited together; do not assort independently. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Recombination frequency"?

options.text: The percentage of offspring showing recombinant phenotypes; used to calculate the distance between linked genes
A cluster of prokaryotic genes under control of a single promoter and operator, transcribed as one polycistronic mRNA (e.g., lac operon, trp operon)
RuBisCO fixes O₂ instead of CO₂, producing a 2-carbon compound that wastes energy; more common at high temperatures
Direct transfer of a phosphate group from a substrate molecule to ADP to form ATP; occurs in glycolysis and the Krebs cycle

quiz_questions.explanation: "Recombination frequency" is defined as: The percentage of offspring showing recombinant phenotypes; used to calculate the distance between linked genes. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Centimorgan (cM)"?

options.text: Genes on the X chromosome; males (XY) express all X-linked alleles; conditions like hemophilia and color blindness are more common in males
Failure of chromosomes to separate properly during meiosis, resulting in gametes with abnormal chromosome numbers (aneuploidy)
A unit of genetic distance: 1 cM = 1% recombination frequency between two genes on the same chromosome
The totality of an organism's chemical reactions; catabolism (breakdown, releases energy) and anabolism (synthesis, requires energy)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Centimorgan (cM)" is defined as: A unit of genetic distance: 1 cM = 1% recombination frequency between two genes on the same chromosome. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Genetic map"?

options.text: A grid used to predict genotype and phenotype ratios in offspring from a particular cross
A technique separating DNA, RNA, or protein fragments by size using an electric field; smaller fragments migrate faster through the agarose or polyacrylamide gel
The main replication enzyme in E. coli; synthesizes DNA 5'→3' by adding nucleotides complementary to the template strand
A diagram showing the relative positions and distances of genes on a chromosome based on recombination frequencies

quiz_questions.explanation: "Genetic map" is defined as: A diagram showing the relative positions and distances of genes on a chromosome based on recombination frequencies. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Parental vs. recombinant"?

options.text: A grid used to predict genotype and phenotype ratios in offspring from a particular cross
Parental types show the same allele combination as the parents; recombinant types show new combinations from crossing over
The main replication enzyme in E. coli; synthesizes DNA 5'→3' by adding nucleotides complementary to the template strand
The molecular machine for translation; composed of large and small subunits containing rRNA and proteins; has A, P, and E sites

quiz_questions.explanation: "Parental vs. recombinant" is defined as: Parental types show the same allele combination as the parents; recombinant types show new combinations from crossing over. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Genes located on the same chromosome that tend to be inherited together; do not assort independently"?

options.text: Central dogma
Law of Segregation
Linked genes
Activation energy (Ea)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Linked genes".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The percentage of offspring showing recombinant phenotypes; used to calculate the distance between linked genes"?

options.text: Genetic drift
Free energy (ΔG)
Tertiary structure
Recombination frequency

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Recombination frequency".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A unit of genetic distance: 1 cM = 1% recombination frequency between two genes on the same chromosome"?

options.text: Nucleus
Photosystem II (P680)
Centimorgan (cM)
Central dogma

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Centimorgan (cM)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A diagram showing the relative positions and distances of genes on a chromosome based on recombination frequencies"?

options.text: Species-area relationship
Genetic map
Codominance
Gel electrophoresis

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Genetic map".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Parental types show the same allele combination as the parents; recombinant types show new combinations from crossing over"?

options.text: Gel electrophoresis
Parental vs. recombinant
Oxidative phosphorylation
microRNA (miRNA)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Parental vs. recombinant".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping is accurate?

options.text: The fossil record shows transitional forms (Tiktaalik between fish and tetrapods), comparative anatomy reveals homologous structures (bat wing, whale flipper, human arm sharing the same bone pattern), molecular biology shows DNA/protein sequence...
Minimum viable population (MVP), effective population size (Ne), and genetic rescue inform conservation management.
Proteins are polymers of amino acids linked by peptide bonds, with structure and function determined by the sequence (primary structure) and folding (secondary, tertiary, quaternary structures).
Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping describe how genes located on the same chromosome tend to be inherited together (linkage), violating Mendel's law of independent assortment.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping?

options.text: Cancer hallmarks include sustained proliferative signaling, evasion of growth suppressors, resistance to cell death, unlimited replication, angiogenesis, and metastasis.
While most mutations are neutral or harmful, some confer advantages (e.g., sickle cell heterozygote advantage against malaria).
Translation is the process by which ribosomes synthesize proteins from mRNA templates.
Recombination frequency between linked genes (calculated from testcross offspring) indicates the distance between genes on a chromosome, measured in centimorgans (cM) or map units.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping?

options.text: The fossil record shows transitional forms (Tiktaalik between fish and tetrapods), comparative anatomy reveals homologous structures (bat wing, whale flipper, human arm sharing the same bone pattern), molecular biology shows DNA/protein sequence...
Genetic mapping constructs a linear order of genes along chromosomes based on recombination frequencies.

Island biogeography theory (MacArthur and Wilson) predicts that species richness on an island reflects a balance between immigration and extinction rates, influenced by island size and distance from the mainland.
Current extinction rates are 100-1,000 times higher than background rates due to habitat destruction, invasive species, overexploitation, pollution, and climate change (HIPPO threats).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping?

options.text: Proteins are polymers of amino acids linked by peptide bonds, with structure and function determined by the sequence (primary structure) and folding (secondary, tertiary, quaternary structures).
Protein denaturation (loss of 3D shape from heat, pH, or chemical changes) destroys function, demonstrating that structure determines function—a central theme in AP Biology.
DNA Replication and Repair describes the semiconservative process by which DNA is copied before cell division.
Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping describe how genes located on the same chromosome tend to be inherited together (linkage), violating Mendel's law of independent assortment.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping?

options.text: Recombination frequency between linked genes (calculated from testcross offspring) indicates the distance between genes on a chromosome, measured in centimorgans (cM) or map units.
DNA polymerase's proofreading (3'→5' exonuclease) and mismatch repair maintain a remarkably low error rate (~1 error per 10⁹ bases).
Natural selection acts on phenotypic variation, favoring traits that increase fitness (reproductive success) in a specific environment.
Current extinction rates are 100-1,000 times higher than background rates due to habitat destruction, invasive species, overexploitation, pollution, and climate change (HIPPO threats).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping?

options.text: Insulin signaling pathway: insulin binding to its receptor triggers a kinase cascade leading to glucose transporter (GLUT4) insertion into cell membranes

Wolf reintroduction into Yellowstone (1995) demonstrating trophic cascades: wolves reduced elk overgrazing, allowing riparian vegetation recovery, which stabilized stream banks and increased beaver populations
Madagascar's unique biodiversity (90% of species found nowhere else) explained by 88 million years of isolation after separating from Africa and India
Thomas Hunt Morgan's Drosophila experiments discovering sex-linked inheritance and gene linkage, proving genes are carried on chromosomes

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping?

options.text: Insulin signaling pathway: insulin binding to its receptor triggers a kinase cascade leading to glucose transporter (GLUT4) insertion into cell membranes
The Human Genome Project using genetic and physical mapping to determine the positions of all ~20,000 human genes across 23 chromosome pairs

Wolf reintroduction into Yellowstone (1995) demonstrating trophic cascades: wolves reduced elk overgrazing, allowing riparian vegetation recovery, which stabilized stream banks and increased beaver populations
Forensic genetics using Mendelian inheritance patterns to establish paternity or identify family relationships from DNA evidence

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Brewing and winemaking using yeast alcohol fermentation to convert glucose to ethanol and CO₂
Agricultural breeding programs using genetic maps to identify and select for markers linked to desirable traits like drought tolerance
Cancer as uncontrolled mitosis: mutations in proto-oncogenes and tumor suppressors enable cells to bypass cell cycle checkpoints
Ecosystem services valuation estimating that pollination by insects is worth $235-577 billion annually globally, providing economic justification for pollinator conservation

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping?

options.text: Agricultural selective breeding applying Mendelian genetics to develop crop varieties with desired traits (disease resistance, yield) over multiple generations
Firefly bioluminescence using ATP to power the oxidation of luciferin, converting chemical energy directly to light with remarkable efficiency
Genetic rescue of the Florida panther: introducing 8 female Texas pumas in 1995 restored genetic diversity and tripled the population from ~25 to ~200 individuals
Thomas Hunt Morgan's Drosophila experiments discovering sex-linked inheritance and gene linkage, proving genes are carried on chromosomes

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping in practice?

options.text: Endosymbiotic theory evidence: mitochondria and chloroplasts have double membranes, own circular DNA, 70S ribosomes, and replicate independently
Coral bleaching when heat stress damages photosystem proteins in symbiotic zooxanthellae, disrupting photosynthesis and causing coral death
The Human Genome Project using genetic and physical mapping to determine the positions of all ~20,000 human genes across 23 chromosome pairs
Cancer as uncontrolled mitosis: mutations in proto-oncogenes and tumor suppressors enable cells to bypass cell cycle checkpoints

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Genetic Linkage and Chromosome Mapping.
